your privacy

We believe everyone has a right to protect their privacy and control the devices they bought and are using. We do not endorse misusing any of the recommended software for ill intent.

Start Here

Your first five moves

Small changes with the biggest impact on your privacy, right now.

🔑

Use a password manager

Create strong, unique passwords for every site. If one website gets hacked, the rest of your accounts stay safe. Read our guide to learn how.
💬

Switch to Signal

Signal is a free messaging app that encrypts everything. This means nobody can read your messages—not even the people who built the app.
🧱

Install uBlock Origin

The best ad blocker available. It stops ads, hidden trackers, and invisible scripts before they even have a chance to load on your screen.
🔐

Enable 2FA everywhere

Two-factor authentication (2FA) means that even if a hacker steals your password, they still need your physical phone to log in to your account.
📱

Audit your app permissions

Check which apps have access to your location, microphone, and camera. Turn off access for any app that doesn't absolutely need it to work.
Learn

Tools

Top-rated software

🔎

Verify us — we mean it

Open your browser's DevTools right now. Check the Network tab, Application → Cookies, and Console. You will find zero third-party requests, zero cookies, and zero analytics. This is a promise, not a policy.

DevTools → Network → ☑ Third-party requests
// Result: empty ✓

DevTools → Application → Cookies
// Result: none ✓
